---
title: "Module 2: Python and SQL Together"
toc: true
toc-location: left
---

SQL handles where the data lives; Python handles what you do with it.

## Slides

[View slides in full screen](intro.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe src="intro.html" width="100%" height="500px" style="border:1px solid #ddd; border-radius:4px;"></iframe>

## Key Takeaways

- Use SQL to filter and aggregate **before** loading into Python
- Avoid pulling entire tables into memory unnecessarily
- SQLAlchemy and DuckDB bridge the two seamlessly
